issue.title: §[res.on.exception.handling] should cover exceptions specified outside a Throws: paragraph

discussion.p: p1 permits a standard library function to throw a type derived from the one it is specified to throw:

blockquote.p: Any of the functions defined in the C++ standard library can report a failure by throwing an exception of a type described in its Throws: paragraph, or of a type derived from a type named in the Throws: paragraph that would be caught by a handler for the base type.

discussion.p: This is worded purely in terms of the function's Throws: paragraph. However, many functions specify the exception they throw outside of a Throws: paragraph. For example, `std::get` on a variant specifies it in an Effects: clause, p7:

blockquote.p: Effects: If `v.index()` is `I`, returns a reference to the object stored in the variant. Otherwise, throws an exception of type `bad_variant_access`.

discussion.p: Read literally, p1 does not grant implementations the ability to throw a derived type, because the exception is described in an Effects: paragraph. I believe this is unintended: the permission should apply whenever the standard specifies that a function throws an exception of a given type, regardless of which clause says so.

blockquote.p: -1- Any of the functions defined in the C++ standard library can report a failure by throwing an exception of a type described in its Throws: paragraphthat the function is specified to throw (whether in a Throws: paragraph or elsewhere in its specification), or of a type derived from a type named in the Throws: paragraphsuch a type that would be caught by a handler () for the base type.
